pattern: [error] <num>#<num>: *<num> upstream timed out (<num>: Connection timed out) while reading response header from upstream, client: <ip>, request: "POST <path> HTTP<path> upstream: "http:<path>
example: 2345#2345: *13 upstream timed out (110: Connection timed out) while reading response header from upstream, client: 203.0.113.5, request: "POST /api/runs HTTP/1.1", upstream: "http://127.0.0.1:7842/api/runs"
root_cause: Backend service is slow or unresponsive, causing NGINX to time out.
next_action: Check the health and load of the backend service (127.0.0.1:7842) by reviewing its logs and metrics.

pattern: [error] <num>#<num>: *<num> connect() failed (<num>: Connection refused) while connecting to upstream, client: <ip>, request: "GET <path> HTTP<path> upstream: "http:<path>
example: 2345#2345: *18 connect() failed (111: Connection refused) while connecting to upstream, client: 192.168.1.100, request: "GET /api/health HTTP/1.1", upstream: "http://127.0.0.1:7842/api/health"
root_cause: Backend service is not running or not accessible by NGINX.
next_action: Verify that the backend service (127.0.0.1:7842) is running and listening on the expected port.

pattern: [crit] <num>#<num>: *<num> SSL_do_handshake() failed (SSL: error:<hex>:SSL routines:ssl3_read_bytes:tlsv1 alert unknown ca) while SSL handshaking, client: <ip>, server: <ip>:<num>
example: 2345#2345: *21 SSL_do_handshake() failed (SSL: error:14094418:SSL routines:ssl3_read_bytes:tlsv1 alert unknown ca) while SSL handshaking, client: 203.0.113.200, server: 0.0.0.0:443
root_cause: SSL handshake failure due to an unknown or untrusted client certificate.
next_action: Investigate the client making the SSL request (203.0.113.200) and ensure they are presenting a valid and trusted certificate, or update the server's trusted CA list if necessary.

pattern: [error] <num>#<num>: *<num> upstream prematurely closed connection while reading response header from upstream, client: <ip>, request: "GET <path> HTTP<path> upstream: "http:<path>
example: 2345#2345: *29 upstream prematurely closed connection while reading response header from upstream, client: 198.51.100.14, request: "GET /api/runs HTTP/1.1", upstream: "http://127.0.0.1:7842/api/runs"
root_cause: Upstream service closed the connection before sending a full response.
next_action: Check the backend service (127.0.0.1:7842) logs for errors or crashes when handling requests for /api/runs.

pattern: [error] <num>#<num>: *<num> open() "<path> failed (2: No such file or directory), client: <ip>, server: _, request: "GET <path> HTTP<path>
example: 2345#2345: *12 open() "/var/www/html/favicon.ico" failed (2: No such file or directory), client: 192.168.1.42, server: _, request: "GET /favicon.ico HTTP/1.1"
root_cause: Requested files do not exist on the server, often favicon.ico or other static assets.
next_action: Verify if the missing files are legitimate assets and either add them or configure NGINX to silently handle these requests if they are benign.

pattern: [warn] <num>#<num>: *<num> an upstream response is buffered to a temporary file <path> while reading upstream, client: <ip>, request: "GET <path> HTTP<path>
example: 2345#2345: *15 an upstream response is buffered to a temporary file /var/cache/nginx/proxy_temp/1/02/0000000001 while reading upstream, client: 198.51.100.9, request: "GET /api/pipelines HTTP/1.1"
root_cause: Upstream response is too large to fit in memory, causing NGINX to buffer to disk.
next_action: Review the configuration of proxy buffering (proxy_buffering, proxy_buffer_size) and consider if the large response is expected or if the backend should return smaller responses.
